steps.title: Substitute t = 3 into the equation
steps.content:
The equation $s = 40 + 2t$ gives the speed $s$, in miles per hour, of the car $t$ seconds after it began to accelerate. To find the speed at $t = 3$, substitute:
$$s = 40 + 2(3) = 40 + 6 = 46$$

steps.title: Identify the answer
steps.content: The speed of the car 3 seconds after it began to accelerate is 46 miles per hour. That matches choice D — D is correct.
